description: Add click-to-zoom modal on kami slots in TemplePanel showing enlarged kami image, name, effect description, and shinto figures present by clan/color
steps: In src/components/TemplePanel.tsx, add React state for the selected kami (useState<KamiType | null>(null))
Add an onClick handler on each .kami-slot.filled div that sets the selected kami state to that temple's kamiType

Add a modal overlay component (inline in the same file) that renders when selectedKami is set. The modal should show: (1) The kami image at a larger size (e.g., 200x200), (2) The kami name prominently, (3) The kami effect text, (4) A list of shinto figures present grouped by clan showing clan color dot + clan name + figure count, (5) An X close button in the top-right corner
Add click-outside-to-close behavior: clicking the backdrop overlay closes the modal

Add CSS styles for the modal in src/App.css: .kami-modal-backdrop (fixed overlay, dark semi-transparent background, z-index high, centered flex), .kami-modal (card-like container, max-width ~400px, padding, border-radius, background matching the game's dark theme), .kami-modal-close (absolute positioned X button top-right)
Use existing KAMI_IMAGES, KAMI_PALETTES, and KAMI_DATA already imported in the file to populate the modal content
Add cursor:pointer styling on .kami-slot.filled to indicate clickability
acceptance_criteria: Clicking a filled kami slot opens a modal overlay
Modal displays enlarged kami image, name, effect, and shinto figures by clan
Clicking X button or clicking outside the modal closes it
Build passes: npx tsc -b && npx vite build
findings: Build passes cleanly. Added useState import from React. Modal uses stopPropagation on its container to prevent backdrop click from closing when clicking inside the modal. CSS uses a fadeIn keyframe animation for smooth backdrop appearance.
